features.summary: Caterpillar reported strong Q1 2021 results with sales up 12% to $11.9B and adjusted EPS of $2.87 vs $1.65 prior year. Operating profit increased 29% to $1.8B with adjusted operating margin of 15.8%, up 230 basis points year-over-year. All three primary segments showed margin expansion, led by Construction Industries which benefited from strong demand in Asia-Pacific (particularly China) and North America residential construction. ME&T free cash flow was robust at $1.7B. The company expects to meet Investor Day targets for both adjusted operating margins and ME&T free cash flow of $4B-$8B in 2021.
features.management_outlook: Management expects normal seasonality in Q2 with continued positive growth across most segments. Construction Industries should benefit from ongoing residential strength in North America and robust China infrastructure spending, though China comparisons become more challenging in H2. Resource Industries is expected to see strong improvement driven by favorable commodity prices and mining CapEx recovery. Energy & Transportation should see continued gradual improvement across applications. The company expects to achieve Investor Day margin targets but anticipates Q2 operating margins to be moderately below Q1 levels due to absorption headwinds and rising material costs.
features.risks: Key risks include potential supply chain disruptions affecting semiconductors, transportation, and raw materials that could impact production and increase costs. The company is developing contingency plans and workarounds but notes the situation remains fluid. Material cost headwinds are expected starting in Q2 due to commodity inflation, though the company is implementing price increases to offset. COVID-19 acceleration in overseas markets presents ongoing uncertainty. There are also concerns about freight cost pressures and potential inability to fully meet improving customer demand if supply chain issues materialize.
features.key_drivers: Primary growth drivers include strong end-user demand recovery, particularly in Construction Industries where sales to users increased 17%. China infrastructure spending and North America residential construction remain key catalysts. Mining sector improvement is driven by favorable commodity prices supporting higher customer CapEx. The company's autonomous solutions and services strategy continue to drive competitive positioning. Dealer inventory increases of $700M provided additional sales benefit, though significant restocking is not expected for the remainder of 2021.
